title: Senior Assistant, Community Health Sciences
description:
Senior Assistant, Community Health Sciences - (2600024N)

**Introduction:**

Aga Khan University's (AKU) Department of Community Health Sciences (CHS) is a preeminent public health institution in the country. The vision of the Chancellor set the direction for the University to take a lead in community development and the creation of an innovative curriculum that emphasizes a balance between patient care and population health. Over the past several decades, CHS has played a major role in leading the development of national level primary health care, public health practice, health systems through evidence-based approach. CHS has established successful models in urban and rural settings in Pakistan emphasizing empowerment - building on the strengths of communities. As part of an international university, CHS collaborates in a global network of outstanding research and teaching institutions including AKU affiliates in East Africa and Central Asia.

CHS has five major sections – Epidemiology & Biostatistics, Health Policy & Management, Population & Reproductive Health, Environmental-Occupational and Climate Change (EOHCC), and Non-Communicable Diseases & Mental Health.

AKU is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to safeguarding and aims to adopt appropriate standards for safeguarding and promoting a respectful relationship with and between all faculty, staff, trainees, volunteers, beneficiaries, wider communities, and other stakeholders with whom it works, including children and vulnerable adults and expects all employees/trainees and partners to share this commitment.

**Responsibilities:**

You will be responsible for:

- provide first-level troubleshooting support for hardware, software, printers, internet connectivity, and peripheral devices within the department
- coordinate with central IT teams for resolution of technical issues and follow-up on service requests
- support installation, configuration, and maintenance of departmental computer systems and related equipment
- assist faculty and staff in the use of online platforms, virtual meeting tools, learning management systems, and departmental applications
- provide technical support during virtual, hybrid, and in-person academic sessions, workshops, seminars, and meetings
- maintain inventory records of IT equipment and coordinate repair/replacement as required
- ensure proper setup and functioning of classroom technology, audiovisual systems, and online teaching platforms
- assist in maintaining departmental data storage, shared drives, and electronic records in coordination with relevant teams
- perform any other duties assigned by the supervisor in line with departmental requirements.

**Requirements:**

You should have:

- Bachelor’s or master’s degree preferred in computer science, Information Technology, or a related field from a recognized institution
- relevant IT certifications (e.g., Microsoft, Cisco, CompTIA) will be an added advantage
- minimum 1–2 years of relevant experience in IT support or systems administration
- experience in troubleshooting hardware, software, and basic network issues
- exposure to enterprise IT environments will be preferred
- strong knowledge of operating systems (Windows, basic Linux)
- understanding of networking concepts (LAN/WAN, IP configuration, troubleshooting)
- proficiency in MS Office and common enterprise applications
- good problem-solving and analytical skills
- effective communication and interpersonal skills
- ability to manage multiple tasks and work under pressure
- strong organizational skills and attention to detail.

Comprehensive employment reference checks will be conducted.

- Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

title: Management Trainee Officer (MTO) – Inventory & Cost Management (ACCA & ICMAP Students only)
description:
**Job Summary:** We are seeking a highly analytical and ambitious MTO to join our Accounts and Inventory Management team. Designed for ambitious professionals, this program will train you in end-to-end inventory control, variance analysis, and cost optimization. You will work closely with warehouse, production, and finance teams to minimize carrying costs and ensure zero stock-outs of raw materials and finished goods.

**Eligibility Criteria**

- **ACCA:** Minimum 9 papers completed.
- **ICMAP:** Passed **Managerial Level -2**.
- **Experience:** Fresh graduates or 0-1 year of relevant internship/practical experience.
- **Technical Skills:** Advanced proficiency in MS Excel (Pivot Tables, VLOOKUP) and fundamental knowledge of ERP systems (SAP/Oracle/ERP).

**Key Responsibilities**

- **Inventory Valuation & Reporting:** Assist in monthly inventory counts, valuation, and reconciliation against the general ledger.
- **Cost Analysis:** Apply financial management principles to analyze inventory carrying costs, holding costs, and material variances (e.g., \(Material \ Variance = (Standard \ Qty \times Standard \ Price) - (Actual \ Qty \times Actual \ Price)\)).
- **Stock Optimization:** Monitor stock levels against production demands to optimize order quantities and prevent overstocking.
- **Data & Analytics:** Maintain and track supply chain KPIs, including stock turnover ratios and Days Sales of Inventory (DSI).
- **Process Improvements:** Support cross-functional initiatives to identify and eliminate slow-moving or obsolete inventory.

**What We Offer**

- **Structured Training:** A 12 to 18-month rotational program spanning procurement, warehousing, and finance.
- **Market Competitive Stipend:** PKR 35,000 to PKR 40,000 per month (depending on qualifications).

title: SQA Trainee
description:
**About Contour**

Contour Software has grown from a dozen people to over 2,000 staff across 3 cities, in less than 14 years.

As a subsidiary of Constellation Software Inc., we are proud to be part of a global enterprise software conglomerate that has grown to become one of the top 10 software companies in the world, with employees and customers in 100+ countries. With a broad-based and ever-growing portfolio of market-leading, vertical-market enterprise solutions covering more than 100 industry domains in predominantly mature markets, CSI's recipe creates the perfect environment for professionals to build fulfilling, long-term careers.

What started as an R&D & Accounting back-office, has progressed into a full-service Global Centre serving all functions and departments, at the divisional as well as operating group/corporate level. Today Contour employees, located in Karachi, Lahore & Islamabad, are serving CSI divisions located in time zones spanning the globe, from Sydney to Vancouver. With the global growth of Constellation as the wind in our sails, we are only just getting started!

FusionSoft, a division of Harris Computer Systems (an operating group of Constellation Software Inc.), specialized in complex back-office operations for the energy sector, specifically targeting commodity management and contract lifecycles.

FusionSoft occupies a niche vertical in the energy industry, moving beyond simple customer billing to handle wholesale and industrial-scale energy management.

**The Position:**

We are looking to hire Interns/FreshGrads to be members of QA teams, executing tasks as part of the test plans for software development. Contribute to QA activities throughout the entire life cycle of the development process, ensuring an understanding of which tasks are needed to support the development team in the production of new features and resolution of defects so that software is delivered to a high level of quality. Contribute to the development of methodologies in relation to quality assurance and follow these methodologies when executing tasks.

The selected **SQA Trainee** would progress from training to apprenticeship, to potentially a full-time permanent employment role in the Contour Software **Karachi**

**/Lahore/Islamabad** office.

**Required Skills & Experience:**

- University degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, and/or related discipline.

- New/Recent/Soon to Graduate Computer Science & Software Engineering graduates are encouraged to apply.

- Familiar with the software development lifecycle and software quality assurance & testing concepts.

- Must have good programming/scripting knowledge and skills. Object-Oriented Programming is preferred.

- Knowledge and exposure to writing and executing SQL queries.

- Knowledge of automation testing tools is a plus.

- Knowledge of different application types/architecture.

- Ability to evaluate a system, understand complexities, identify weaknesses/bottlenecks.

- Must have good analytical and problem-solving skills.

- Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English.

**Key Competencies:**

- Attention to detail

- Data collection, management, and analysis

- Problem analysis and problem-solving

- Planning and organizing

- Judgment

- Decision-making

- Teamwork

- Ability to think and work independently

- Energetic and a "can-do” attitude

**Suitable for someone who:**

Is passionate about ensuring the best quality software is delivered, have an analytical mind and thinks about different ways to use the system and is willing to ask questions and document the answers in order to understand how the system should work, whilst identifying areas that may not behave as expected.

***Disclaimer:*** *At Contour, we attribute our success to the unique contributions of our diverse staff. We’re committed to fostering a culture of respect that thrives on the varied perspectives and experiences of all individuals we recruit, employ, promote, and compensate. Since day one, we’ve adhered to a policy that champions a work environment honoring the worth and dignity of each person while being free from all forms of employment discrimination.*

description:
Assistant Manager (Programmes), Brain and Mind Institute - (260002DB)

**Assistant Manager (Programmes), Brain and Mind Institute**

**Entity**

:
Office of the Provost

**Location**

:
Karachi, Pakistan

**Introduction**

:
Aga Khan University, chartered in 1983 as Pakistan's first private international university, is committed to the provision of education, research, and health care of international standards relevant to Pakistan and the region. The affiliated Aga Khan University Hospital provides state-of-the-art clinical facilities and well-equipped diagnostic services. The University currently has teaching sites in Pakistan, East Africa, the United Kingdom, and Afghanistan, and is a major component of the Aga Khan Development Network.

As an equal opportunity employer, AKU believes in promoting a diverse and inclusive culture and is committed to adopt appropriate standards for safeguarding and promoting a respectful relationship with and between diverse workforce of its faculty, staff, trainees, volunteers, beneficiaries, wider communities, and other stakeholders with whom it works, including children and vulnerable adults and expects all employees/trainees and partners to share this commitment.

The Brain and Mind Institute (BMI) is a newly minted entity at the Aga Khan University (AKU), with key hubs in Nairobi (for East Africa) and Karachi (for South and Central Asia). The BMI seeks to build capacity in the domains of mental health and neuroscience. In collaboration with partners, the Institute is advancing research leadership capacity and support service delivery across East Africa and South Asia, and other regions served by the AKU. The Institute supports a hub for scholarship exchange and action on mental health issues, as well as educational, clinical, and community-based programs. BMI’s research focuses on the context of today’s youth in Africa and South Asia, while our programmatic offerings address mental health related and brain health issues, including stigma and resiliency. A significant area of interest is the implementation of science-based programming in hospital and community settings that translate new discoveries into prevention and treatment programs.

This is a grant-funded contractual position.

**Responsibilities**

:
You will be responsible to:

- prepare, review, and update programme documents, templates, and communication materials
- develop and maintain Standard Operating Procedures for administrative and programmatic processes to ensure standardised practices across sites
- ensure document filing systems are organised, current, and accessible to the team
- develop and maintain project progress tracking tools including Gantt Charts, activity trackers, and milestone logs
- ensure implementation of goals and objectives according to set timelines, flagging delays or risks to the Supervisor
- regularly review contributions to programme plans and coordinate activities with the needs of the programme at large
- provide overall administrative and operational support to the Supervisor and programme team
- coordinate logistics for field activities, trainings, workshops, and team events
- support procurement processes for programme goods and services in line with institutional procedures
- maintain and update the master programme workplan on a regular basis, incorporating team inputs and reflecting actual progress
- coordinate with field teams, partners, and consultants to ensure activity timelines are adhered to
- prepare consolidated workplan progress updates for review by the Supervisor prior to donor or institutional submission
- serve as the primary day-to-day point of contact for implementing partners, community stakeholders, and field offices
- facilitate regular coordination meetings with partners, document minutes, and track action points to completion
- maintain organised records of partnership agreements, MoUs, and stakeholder communication logs
- escalate collaborator issues or challenges to the Supervisor
- track activity-level expenditures against approved budgets and flag deviations to the Supervisor
- maintain expense logs and support preparation of financial reports and liquidations for programme activities
- coordinate with the finance team on procurement requests, payment processing, and vendor documentation
- manage day-to-day HR administrative processes for field and programme staff, including attendance, leave tracking, and timesheet management
- coordinate onboarding logistics for new programme staff, including documentation, induction scheduling, and equipment setup
- liaise with HR on contractual and administrative matters for project staff, escalating issues to the Supervisor as needed
- compile and draft regular programme progress reports consolidating updates from all team members and field sites
- maintain a reporting calendar and proactively follow up with team members to ensure inputs are received on time
- prepare first drafts of narrative sections for donor reports and internal updates for review by the Supervisor.

**Requirements:**
You should have:

- a minimum of Master’s degree in Public Health, Health Management, Management or related field preferred
- training or certification in project management (e.g., project cycle management, PMP, or equivalent) is an advantage
- at least two years of relevant experience in programme coordination, research administration, or a related role
- demonstrated experience in developing and maintaining project tracking tools, SOPs, and administrative systems.
- knowledge of research principles, concepts, practices, and methods.
- knowledge of research paper writing.
- prior experience coordinating implementation activities across multiple field sites.
- experience managing partner relationships and facilitating coordination meetings.
- familiarity with grant management systems and activity-level budget tracking.
- experience handling HR administrative processes for field or project staff.
- computer literacy and strong mathematical, analytical, and research skills.
- excellent interpersonal and written and verbal communication skills.
- knowledge of both qualitative and quantitative research principles, concepts, practices, and methods.
- ability to develop and implement Standard Operating Procedures and administrative systems.
- strong workplan management and activity tracking skills, with ability to flag risks and variances proactively.
- ability to coordinate across multiple stakeholders and maintain clear communication without senior escalation.
- familiarity with institutional financial and HR compliance procedures, including procurement and leave management.
- ability to draft clear and accurate programme progress reports for internal and external audiences.
- ability to independently facilitate team meetings, document decisions, and follow up on action points.

Comprehensive employment reference checks will be conducted.

title: Senior Consultants - DevOps - Financial Accounting & Advisory Services - Pakistan
description:
Location: Karachi

Other locations: Primary Location Only

Salary: Competitive

Date: Jun 11, 2026

## **Job description**

Requisition ID: 1717321

We are the AI Hub delivering advisory services focused on data architecture and data-driven transformation.
We work closely with client and internal stakeholders, including cybersecurity and data protection teams, data analysts, and data scientists to ensure use cases have a solid foundation and deliverables meet expectations.

We support organizations in designing and delivering next-generation data platforms to accelerate analytics, decision-making, and innovation. Our work typically includes assessing architecture maturity, defining target-state data platform blueprints, and building pragmatic roadmaps that cover governance, protection, migration, and cloud enablement. We deliver in a consulting environment that requires strong stakeholder management, delivery discipline, compliance with regulatory requirements, and coordination across service lines when integrated delivery is needed.

Sound interesting? Well this is just the beginning. Because whenever you join, however long you stay, the exceptional EY experience lasts a lifetime.

**The opportunity**

As a Senior DevOps Engineer in the AI Hub, you will design, automate, secure, and operate cloud and hybrid platforms for AI-driven applications. You will own CI/CD, infrastructure as code, observability, release governance, and production readiness so teams can move from prototype to production safely and repeatably. Azure is the primary platform, with client on-prem/hybrid environments a key delivery context.

**Your key responsibilities**

You will partner with developers, AI/data engineers, product owners, security teams, and client stakeholders to architect solutions and standardize reliable delivery.

**Skills and attributes for success**

- Own an Azure-first DevOps/developer platform: reusable templates, pipeline blueprints, environment patterns, and deployment standards

- Enable teams to ship quickly with CI/CD, IaC, observability, and security controls built into the “golden path” for new services

- Partner on solution/platform architecture (hosting choices, environment design, integration patterns, security, scalability, reliability, production readiness)

- Build and optimize CI/CD for front-end, back-end, data, and AI/ML services (Azure DevOps/GitHub Actions or equivalent)

- Define and evolve IaC modules and standards (Bicep/Terraform/ARM)

- Deploy and run services on Azure (App Service, Functions, Static Web Apps, Container Apps, AKS) with sound release and environment management (approvals, gates, rollback)

- Embed security by design and in pipelines (Entra ID, managed identities, RBAC, Key Vault, private endpoints; scanning/policy checks; secure releases)

- Establish operational readiness: monitoring/alerting, dashboards, runbooks, and incident response (Azure Monitor/App Insights/Log Analytics)

- Help productionize AI/GenAI workloads (LLMOps/MLOps patterns, quality gates, monitoring)

- Support cloud/hybrid/on-prem deployments (secure connectivity, network constraints, integration with client infrastructure)

- Automate ops with scripting (PowerShell/Bash/Python/Azure CLI) and drive reliability/performance/cost improvements (RCA, capacity, optimization)

- Collaborate across engineering, security, product, and clients; contribute runbooks, standards, and documentation that raise delivery quality

**To qualify for the role you must have**

- 7+ years in DevOps/platform or cloud engineering (infrastructure automation)

- Strong hands-on Azure experience in production/enterprise environments

- CI/CD pipeline delivery (Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions, or similar)

- Developer enablement via reusable platform patterns, templates, and deployment standards (internal developer platforms a plus)

- Infrastructure as code (Bicep, Terraform, ARM, or equivalent)

- Deploy/operate web apps, APIs, containers, and serverless on Azure (App Service, Functions, Container Apps, AKS, Key Vault, Monitor/Log Analytics, networking)

- Docker and container patterns; Kubernetes/AKS strongly preferred

- Observability/monitoring, alerting, incident response, and production troubleshooting

- Enterprise security and hybrid/on-prem constraints (identity/RBAC, secrets, private networking, controlled releases, VPN/private endpoints)

- Comfortable in iterative delivery teams (PoCs, MVPs, and production)

**Ideally you’ll also have**

- Microsoft certifications (e.g., AZ-400 DevOps Engineer Expert)

- Deep AKS/Kubernetes operations (Helm, ingress, registries, container security)

- Reusable infrastructure modules (Terraform and/or Bicep)

- Azure governance (Landing Zones, Policy, Defender for Cloud, Sentinel)

- Regulated/security-sensitive environments (privacy, auditability, access control, data residency)

- AI/GenAI platform experience (RAG, vector DBs, prompt/eval pipelines, monitoring)

- On-prem/hybrid infrastructure (Windows/Linux, enterprise networking, VPN/private connectivity, modernization)

- FinOps/cost optimization (monitoring, budgets, controls)

- SAP/ERP and enterprise system integrations (Azure or hybrid)

**What we look for**

We are interested in entrepreneurs who have the confidence to develop and promote a brand new strategic vision both internally and externally. You will be business savvy with a passion for innovation as well as the motivation to create your own EY journey.

**What we offer**

We offer a competitive compensation package where you’ll be rewarded based on performance and recognized for the value you bring to our business. Plus, we offer:

- **Continuous learning:** You’ll develop the mindset and skills to navigate whatever comes next.
- **Success as defined by you:** We’ll provide the tools and flexibility, so you can make a meaningful impact, your way.
- **Transformative leadership:** We’ll give you the insights, coaching and confidence to be the leader the world needs.
- **Diverse and inclusive culture:** You’ll be embraced for who you are and empowered to use your voice to help others find theirs.

**If you can demonstrate that you meet the criteria above, please contact us as soon as possible.**

**The exceptional EY experience. It’s yours to build.**

**Research Associate, CITRIC Centre for Clinical Best Practices (CCBP), Deans Office**

**Introduction:**

The Center for Clinical Best Practices (CCBP) has been working in collaboration with the U.S GRADE (Grading of Recommendations, Assessment, Development and Evaluation) Network to develop evidence-based guidelines and care pathways to standardize and improve health outcomes and quality of health care in Low and Middle-Income Countries. The center will channelize and consolidate resources and efforts to bring in the much needed standardized, high-quality evidence-based care to the communities where AKU exists, taking into account the local challenges and opportunities.

As an equal opportunity employer, AKU believes in promoting a diverse and inclusive culture and is committed to adopt appropriate standards for safeguarding and promoting a respectful relationship with and between diverse workforce of its faculty, staff, trainees, volunteers, beneficiaries, wider communities, and other stakeholders with whom it works, including children and vulnerable adults and expects all employees/trainees and partners to share this commitment.

The Aga Khan University invites applications for the position of Research Associate in the CITRIC CCBP.

**Responsibilities:**

Reporting to Manager, CCBP, you will be responsible to assist the research team in conducting preliminary literature reviews, collecting and analyzing research data, scientific writing, completing manuscripts / grant applications, proofreading and editing any research documents and providing support to the Manager, CCBP to ensure smooth operations of the Centre.

Specifically, you will be responsible for:

- working with Sections and Departments to coordinate guidelines and manual writing. Completion of chapters based on created guidelines
- writing, submitting, keeping track of deadlines. Tracking progress of guideline creation using GANTT Charts. Writing the guidelines for submission to Section Heads and Chairs, as needed. Completion of guidelines for each section by stipulated timeline
- working with faculty members, clinicians, and patient advocates to develop patient-centered educational content for manuals, digital applications, and other patient-facing resource
- assessing the research evidence and assist the panel of experts in creating recommendations and guidelines
- providing recommendations and create guidelines after careful consideration of data analysed
- keeping close coordination with CCBP Associate Directors, Chairs and Section Heads
- collaborating with multidisciplinary teams, including clinicians, patient advocates, researchers, and digital designers, to understand user needs and improve educational resources
- supporting the development, testing, evaluation, and refinement of patient education materials and digital health content
- timely engagement, support, and updates to the key stakeholder

**Requirements:**

You should have:

- MBBS/BSN/BDS or Allied Health degree with a strong interest or secondary degree in a public health discipline
- scientific paper writing, chapter writing experience. Experience in health science research
- excellent interpersonal and communication skills and ability to collaborate with others (across geographies) on projects and investigations
- experience in submission of international and national grants
experience in patient-centered outcomes research, patient education, or health literacy initiatives will be an advantage
- prior experience of development, maintenance of guideline creation is preferred
- strong analytical, observational, and problem-solving skills
- strong scientific writing skills
- experience with guidelines writing preferable
- knowledge of GRADE methodology preferable

- ability to effectively work independently and establish harmonious working relations in the workplace
- effective time management to prioritize assignments and bring to completion as per timelines

description:
Assistant Manager (Programmes), Brain and Mind Institute - (260002DB)

**Assistant Manager (Programmes), Brain and Mind Institute**

**Entity**

:
Office of the Provost

**Location**

:
Karachi, Pakistan

**Introduction**

:
Aga Khan University, chartered in 1983 as Pakistan's first private international university, is committed to the provision of education, research, and health care of international standards relevant to Pakistan and the region. The affiliated Aga Khan University Hospital provides state-of-the-art clinical facilities and well-equipped diagnostic services. The University currently has teaching sites in Pakistan, East Africa, the United Kingdom, and Afghanistan, and is a major component of the Aga Khan Development Network.

As an equal opportunity employer, AKU believes in promoting a diverse and inclusive culture and is committed to adopt appropriate standards for safeguarding and promoting a respectful relationship with and between diverse workforce of its faculty, staff, trainees, volunteers, beneficiaries, wider communities, and other stakeholders with whom it works, including children and vulnerable adults and expects all employees/trainees and partners to share this commitment.

The Brain and Mind Institute (BMI) is a newly minted entity at the Aga Khan University (AKU), with key hubs in Nairobi (for East Africa) and Karachi (for South and Central Asia). The BMI seeks to build capacity in the domains of mental health and neuroscience. In collaboration with partners, the Institute is advancing research leadership capacity and support service delivery across East Africa and South Asia, and other regions served by the AKU. The Institute supports a hub for scholarship exchange and action on mental health issues, as well as educational, clinical, and community-based programs. BMI’s research focuses on the context of today’s youth in Africa and South Asia, while our programmatic offerings address mental health related and brain health issues, including stigma and resiliency. A significant area of interest is the implementation of science-based programming in hospital and community settings that translate new discoveries into prevention and treatment programs.

This is a grant-funded contractual position.

**Responsibilities**

:
You will be responsible to:

- prepare, review, and update programme documents, templates, and communication materials
- develop and maintain Standard Operating Procedures for administrative and programmatic processes to ensure standardised practices across sites
- ensure document filing systems are organised, current, and accessible to the team
- develop and maintain project progress tracking tools including Gantt Charts, activity trackers, and milestone logs
- ensure implementation of goals and objectives according to set timelines, flagging delays or risks to the Supervisor
- regularly review contributions to programme plans and coordinate activities with the needs of the programme at large
- provide overall administrative and operational support to the Supervisor and programme team
- coordinate logistics for field activities, trainings, workshops, and team events
- support procurement processes for programme goods and services in line with institutional procedures
- maintain and update the master programme workplan on a regular basis, incorporating team inputs and reflecting actual progress
- coordinate with field teams, partners, and consultants to ensure activity timelines are adhered to
- prepare consolidated workplan progress updates for review by the Supervisor prior to donor or institutional submission
- serve as the primary day-to-day point of contact for implementing partners, community stakeholders, and field offices
- facilitate regular coordination meetings with partners, document minutes, and track action points to completion
- maintain organised records of partnership agreements, MoUs, and stakeholder communication logs
- escalate collaborator issues or challenges to the Supervisor
- track activity-level expenditures against approved budgets and flag deviations to the Supervisor
- maintain expense logs and support preparation of financial reports and liquidations for programme activities
- coordinate with the finance team on procurement requests, payment processing, and vendor documentation
- manage day-to-day HR administrative processes for field and programme staff, including attendance, leave tracking, and timesheet management
- coordinate onboarding logistics for new programme staff, including documentation, induction scheduling, and equipment setup
- liaise with HR on contractual and administrative matters for project staff, escalating issues to the Supervisor as needed
- compile and draft regular programme progress reports consolidating updates from all team members and field sites
- maintain a reporting calendar and proactively follow up with team members to ensure inputs are received on time

**Requirements:**

- a minimum of Master’s degree in Public Health, Health Management, Management or related field preferred

The affiliated Aga Khan University Hospital provides state-of-the-art clinical facilities and well-equipped diagnostic services. The University currently has teaching sites in Pakistan, East Africa, the United Kingdom, and Afghanistan, and is a major component of the Aga Khan Development Network.

As an equal opportunity employer, AKU believes in promoting a diverse and inclusive culture and is committed to adopt appropriate standards for safeguarding and promoting a respectful relationship with and between diverse workforce of its faculty, staff, trainees, volunteers, beneficiaries, wider communities, and other stakeholders with whom it works, including children and vulnerable adults and expects all employees/trainees and partners to share this commitment.

The Brain and Mind Institute (BMI) is a newly minted entity at the Aga Khan University (AKU), with key hubs in Nairobi (for East Africa) and Karachi (for South and Central Asia). The BMI seeks to build capacity in the domains of mental health and neuroscience. In collaboration with partners, the Institute is advancing research leadership capacity and support service delivery across East Africa and South Asia, and other regions served by the AKU. The Institute supports a hub for scholarship exchange and action on mental health issues, as well as educational, clinical, and community-based programs. BMI’s research focuses on the context of today’s youth in Africa and South Asia, while our programmatic offerings address mental health related and brain health issues, including stigma and resiliency. A significant area of interest is the implementation of science-based programming in hospital and community settings that translate new discoveries into prevention and treatment programs.

This is a grant-funded contractual position.

**Responsibilities**

:
You will be responsible to:

- develop research objectives and design research projects, studies, and proposals
- develop and refine data collection instruments ensuring scientific validity and cultural appropriateness
- develop Standard Operating Procedures for research and data management processes
- respond to research questions, troubleshoot problems, and propose and implement changes to methods and procedures
- formulate and conduct scientific experiments and data collection activities
- supervise and monitor data collection activities to ensure adherence to protocols, consistency, and completeness
- perform statistical data analysis and evaluate and interpret research results
- replicate, evaluate, and refine research strategies; recommend and implement improvements
- contribute to and write research findings for publications, papers, presentations, grant applications, and other documents
- assist with grant and manuscript submissions
- plan and maintain records of publications
- maintain and track all research ethics and IRB submissions, approvals, amendments, renewals, and adverse event reporting
- ensure research activities are conducted in compliance with approved protocols, institutional policies
- maintain a comprehensive research register covering all active studies, protocols, and approval statuses
- serve as the day-to-day point of contact for ethics-related queries from the team, escalating issues to the Supervisor as appropriate
- design, maintain, and oversee research databases, ensuring data security, integrity, and appropriate access controls
- implement and monitor data quality
- conduct regular data quality field monitoring and enforce protocols and produce QA reports summarizing findings for the Supervisor
- coordinate day-to-day tasks and schedules of research staff, enumerators, and data collectors
- guide, train, and advise junior researchers and field staff on research methods, protocols, and data collection tools
- conduct field visit of active sites to monitor progress of research staff against assigned tasks and timelines, flagging performance issues to the Supervisor
- support research team training and tool orientation
- compile research data summaries, indicator updates, and analytical inputs for inclusion in program progress reports
- prepare first-draft research narrative sections for donor reports, annual reviews, and internal institutional submissions
- liaise with the AM - Programs for integration into overall program reporting
- support the establishment and development of the regional research teams
- provide administrative and organizational support to the program lead on research-related matters
- contribute to and arrange dissemination activities at the regional & international level.

**Requirements:**
You should have:

- a master's degree with major coursework in Computer Science, Statistical Analysis, Epidemiology, Research Methodology, or a related field
- postgraduate training in data management, biostatistics, or research governance is an advantage
- at least two years of relevant experience in programme coordination, research administration, or a related role
- prior experience coordinating research staff or field data collection teams
- experience managing or contributing to research ethics and IRB compliance processes
- experience implementing or maintaining data quality assurance systems across a multi-site research programme
- demonstrated experience in quantitative data collection, database management, and statistical analysis
- knowledge of research principles, concepts, practices, and methods
- knowledge of statistical concepts and methods and their application to research analysis, measurement, testing, and evaluation
- knowledge of research paper writing and familiarity with grant and manuscript submission processes
- demonstrated contribution to donor or institutional research progress reports.

Comprehensive employment reference checks will be conducted.

Organisation: Aga Khan Hospital & Medical College

description:

description:
**Position Title: AI Consultant – Computer Vision & Deep Learning**

**Employment Type: Consultancy Contract** (Potential to transition into a Full-Time role)

**About the Role:**
We are seeking an experienced **AI Consultant** to support the development of an AI-powered image enhancement platform for a US-based client. The platform is focused on leveraging advanced AI technologies to improve, optimize, and enhance digital images, similar in concept to AutoEnhance.ai.

This is a senior-level consultancy position ideal for someone with strong expertise in Computer Vision, Deep Learning, and Machine Learning who can provide technical leadership, mentorship, and strategic guidance to our engineering team.

The consultant will work closely with our existing AI Engineer and play a key role in ensuring the successful delivery of the project.

**Key Responsibilities:**

- Provide technical leadership and strategic direction for the AI development team.
- Design, review, and optimize Computer Vision and Deep Learning solutions.
- Guide the development and deployment of AI-powered image enhancement models.
- Train, fine-tune, and evaluate custom deep learning models.
- Review model performance and recommend improvements for accuracy, efficiency, and scalability.
- Mentor and support AI engineers throughout the development lifecycle.
- Assist in selecting appropriate AI/ML frameworks, architectures, and best practices.
- Collaborate with stakeholders to translate business requirements into technical solutions.
- Identify technical risks and propose mitigation strategies.
- Conduct code, architecture, and model reviews to maintain high-quality standards.

**Required Qualifications:**

- Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Data Science, or a related field.
- Proven experience in Computer Vision, AI/ML, and Deep Learning.
- Strong understanding of image processing and image enhancement techniques.
- Hands-on experience training, fine-tuning, and deploying custom deep learning models.
- Experience with frameworks such as:
- PyTorch
- TensorFlow / Keras
- OpenCV
- Scikit-learn
- Experience working with CNNs, GANs, Diffusion Models, or other advanced computer vision architectures.
- Strong understanding of model evaluation, optimization, and performance tuning.
- Ability to provide mentorship and technical guidance to engineering teams.
- Excellent communication and problem-solving skills.

**Preferred Qualifications:**

- Experience building AI products related to image enhancement, restoration, upscaling, or visual quality improvement.
- Experience working with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud.
- Experience deploying AI models into production environments.
- Previous consulting or technical advisory experience.
- Hands-on experience training, fine-tuning, or working with advanced image enhancement and restoration models such as NAFNet, Real Estate Enhancement Models, AHDRNet, HDRFeat, MSPENet, or similar Computer Vision architectures.
- Experience with HDR image reconstruction, image denoising, exposure correction, image restoration, and photo enhancement techniques.
- Familiarity with state-of-the-art research and emerging trends in Computer Vision and image enhancement.

**Engagement Details:**

- Consultancy-based engagement with flexible working arrangements.
- Opportunity to work on a cutting-edge AI product for a US-based client.
- Potential to transition into a full-time position based on project needs and performance.
- Working hours, expected commitment, and compensation will be discussed and agreed upon mutually.

Pay: Rs300,000.00 - Rs350,000.00 per month

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Karachi

title: Senior Consultants - DevOps - Financial Accounting & Advisory Services - Pakistan
description:
Location: Karachi

Other locations: Primary Location Only

Salary: Competitive

Date: Jun 11, 2026

## **Job description**

Requisition ID: 1717321

We are the AI Hub delivering advisory services focused on data architecture and data-driven transformation.
We work closely with client and internal stakeholders, including cybersecurity and data protection teams, data analysts, and data scientists to ensure use cases have a solid foundation and deliverables meet expectations.

We support organizations in designing and delivering next-generation data platforms to accelerate analytics, decision-making, and innovation. Our work typically includes assessing architecture maturity, defining target-state data platform blueprints, and building pragmatic roadmaps that cover governance, protection, migration, and cloud enablement. We deliver in a consulting environment that requires strong stakeholder management, delivery discipline, compliance with regulatory requirements, and coordination across service lines when integrated delivery is needed.

Sound interesting? Well this is just the beginning. Because whenever you join, however long you stay, the exceptional EY experience lasts a lifetime.

**The opportunity**

As a Senior DevOps Engineer in the AI Hub, you will design, automate, secure, and operate cloud and hybrid platforms for AI-driven applications. You will own CI/CD, infrastructure as code, observability, release governance, and production readiness so teams can move from prototype to production safely and repeatably. Azure is the primary platform, with client on-prem/hybrid environments a key delivery context.

**Your key responsibilities**

You will partner with developers, AI/data engineers, product owners, security teams, and client stakeholders to architect solutions and standardize reliable delivery.

**Skills and attributes for success**

- Own an Azure-first DevOps/developer platform: reusable templates, pipeline blueprints, environment patterns, and deployment standards

- Enable teams to ship quickly with CI/CD, IaC, observability, and security controls built into the “golden path” for new services

- Partner on solution/platform architecture (hosting choices, environment design, integration patterns, security, scalability, reliability, production readiness)

- Build and optimize CI/CD for front-end, back-end, data, and AI/ML services (Azure DevOps/GitHub Actions or equivalent)

- Define and evolve IaC modules and standards (Bicep/Terraform/ARM)

- Deploy and run services on Azure (App Service, Functions, Static Web Apps, Container Apps, AKS) with sound release and environment management (approvals, gates, rollback)

- Embed security by design and in pipelines (Entra ID, managed identities, RBAC, Key Vault, private endpoints; scanning/policy checks; secure releases)

- Establish operational readiness: monitoring/alerting, dashboards, runbooks, and incident response (Azure Monitor/App Insights/Log Analytics)

- Help productionize AI/GenAI workloads (LLMOps/MLOps patterns, quality gates, monitoring)

- Support cloud/hybrid/on-prem deployments (secure connectivity, network constraints, integration with client infrastructure)

- Automate ops with scripting (PowerShell/Bash/Python/Azure CLI) and drive reliability/performance/cost improvements (RCA, capacity, optimization)

- Collaborate across engineering, security, product, and clients; contribute runbooks, standards, and documentation that raise delivery quality

**To qualify for the role you must have**

- 7+ years in DevOps/platform or cloud engineering (infrastructure automation)

- Strong hands-on Azure experience in production/enterprise environments

- CI/CD pipeline delivery (Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions, or similar)

- Developer enablement via reusable platform patterns, templates, and deployment standards (internal developer platforms a plus)

- Infrastructure as code (Bicep, Terraform, ARM, or equivalent)

- Deploy/operate web apps, APIs, containers, and serverless on Azure (App Service, Functions, Container Apps, AKS, Key Vault, Monitor/Log Analytics, networking)

- Docker and container patterns; Kubernetes/AKS strongly preferred

- Observability/monitoring, alerting, incident response, and production troubleshooting

- Enterprise security and hybrid/on-prem constraints (identity/RBAC, secrets, private networking, controlled releases, VPN/private endpoints)

- Comfortable in iterative delivery teams (PoCs, MVPs, and production)

**Ideally you’ll also have**

- Microsoft certifications (e.g., AZ-400 DevOps Engineer Expert)

- Deep AKS/Kubernetes operations (Helm, ingress, registries, container security)

- Reusable infrastructure modules (Terraform and/or Bicep)

- Azure governance (Landing Zones, Policy, Defender for Cloud, Sentinel)

- Regulated/security-sensitive environments (privacy, auditability, access control, data residency)

- AI/GenAI platform experience (RAG, vector DBs, prompt/eval pipelines, monitoring)

- On-prem/hybrid infrastructure (Windows/Linux, enterprise networking, VPN/private connectivity, modernization)

- FinOps/cost optimization (monitoring, budgets, controls)

- SAP/ERP and enterprise system integrations (Azure or hybrid)

**What we look for**

We are interested in entrepreneurs who have the confidence to develop and promote a brand new strategic vision both internally and externally. You will be business savvy with a passion for innovation as well as the motivation to create your own EY journey.

**What we offer**

We offer a competitive compensation package where you’ll be rewarded based on performance and recognized for the value you bring to our business. Plus, we offer:

- **Continuous learning:** You’ll develop the mindset and skills to navigate whatever comes next.
- **Success as defined by you:** We’ll provide the tools and flexibility, so you can make a meaningful impact, your way.
- **Transformative leadership:** We’ll give you the insights, coaching and confidence to be the leader the world needs.
- **Diverse and inclusive culture:** You’ll be embraced for who you are and empowered to use your voice to help others find theirs.

**If you can demonstrate that you meet the criteria above, please contact us as soon as possible.**

**The exceptional EY experience. It’s yours to build.**

**EY | Building a better working world**

EY exists to build a better working world, helping to create long-term value for clients, people and society and build trust in the capital markets.

Enabled by data and technology, diverse EY teams in over 150 countries provide trust through assurance and help clients grow, transform and operate.

Working across assurance, consulting, law, strategy, tax and transactions, EY teams ask better questions to find new answers for the complex issues facing our world today.

title: Sr. Software Engineer
description:
- **Job Summary** **:**

As a Sr. Developer, you’ll take ownership of the end-to-end design, implementation, and optimization of our core AI operator modules/kernels. You will be working with diverse cross-functional teams, enhance the performance, reliability, and scalability of our edge AI processors. Your contributions will directly impact our ability to deliver best-in-class AI/ML performance across our product portfolio.

**What You’ll Do:**
- **Design and Development:**
- Design and Development of our AI operator modules/kernels to maximize performance and efficiency on our custom ISA-based edge AI processors.
- Understand mathematics of AI operators and come up with solutions towards implementing them in our hardware.
- Understand overall architecture, evaluate cutting-edge methodologies to enhance AI operator performance in both currently released and future products.
- Develop, optimize, and maintain high-performance kernel-level code in C/C++ (and assembly when needed) for AI/ML operators.
- Implement innovative features that streamline system processes, reduce latency.
- Enthusiastic in exploring overall SW stack to come up with innovative solutions.
- **Performance Optimization & Debugging:**
- Utilize advanced profiling and debugging tools (e.g., GDB, trace analyzers) to diagnose and resolve performance bottlenecks.
- Drive continuous performance improvements through rigorous testing and iterative optimization strategies.
- **Cross-Functional Collaboration:**
- Work closely with hardware architects, compiler teams, and application developers to ensure seamless integration of kernel components with higher-level systems.
- **Innovation & Continuous Learning:**
- Stay current on emerging trends in AI operator development, and specialized ISA optimizations.
- Champion a culture of innovation by proposing and implementing forward-thinking solutions that drive our technology forward.
- **What We’re Looking For:**
- **Educational Background:**
- Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Electrical/Computer Engineering, or a related field; a Ph.D. is a strong plus.
- **Proven Expertise:**
- 5+ years of hands-on experience in kernel or low-level systems software development, with demonstrable contributions to production-quality code.
- Experience with AI/ML operator development or specialized compiler optimizations.
- Exposure to hardware/software co-design and emerging trends in AI hardware acceleration.
- Deep knowledge of OS internals, memory management, process scheduling, and performance tuning.
- Expertise in system-level programming using C/C++ and proficiency in assembly language for specialized ISA implementations.
- Strong familiarity with kernel debugging and profiling tools (e.g., GDB, Gprof, or equivalent).
Track record of optimizing software performance in complex, high-performance computing environments.
